title: LLM attention-tail scheduler visibility decision gate
hypothesis: The existing LLM attention-tail campaign can produce a decision-useful scheduler visibility report by deriving per-token scaling and a bottleneck recommendation from softmax occupancy, backpressure, and dependency-wait counters.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does the attention-tail benchmark show a softmax datapath bottleneck, scheduler overlap bottleneck, or insufficient evidence for architecture work?
include: per-token latency derived from model sequence length
softmax occupancy, backpressure, and dependency wait counters
a review-package recommendation for the next architecture action

expected_benefit: turns attention-tail evidence into an explicit architecture decision gate
prevents launching softmax buffering or pipeline work when counters do not show pressure
keeps the next architecture proposal grounded in measured scheduler behavior
risks: derived per-token latency depends on model-id naming for this benchmark slice
zero backpressure in the current simulator may mean either no bottleneck or insufficient stress
